photography|WD=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}} enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers at {{ill|Open-Air Bath|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856214}} facilities nationwide. The secretly filmed videos were shared among the group’s members, and screening events were even held for viewership.[https://jref.com/threads/onsen-voyeurism-17-arrested-over-decades-of-hot-spring-peeping.509953/ Crime - Onsen voyeurism: 17 arrested over decades of hot spring peeping | Japan Reference] Starting with the arrest of three men—including the ringleader referred to as Man A—in December 2021, a total of 16 men have been arrested by the {{ill|Shizuoka Prefectural Police|de|Polizei von Shizuoka|ja|静岡県警察|zh|静冈县警察|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefectural Police|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663257}} as of February 2023 (see {{ill|Arrest|en|Arrest|WD=Q1403016|lt=Later|1=Arrest}}). Although the full scope of the operation remains unclear, over 80 facilities have been affected by the secret filming, more than 1,200 items have been confiscated, and the group is believed to number over 100 members{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/sbs/306002 |title=「沖縄のぞく46都道府県で1万人以上撮った」“盗撮のカリスマ”が供述　マニアグループは総勢100人以上に…SNSやオフ会などで増えたか |accessdate=2023-02-01 |date=2023-02-01 |website=静岡新聞SBS}}. == Summary == === Affected Locations === * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Ibaraki|ja|茨城県の露天風呂|zh|茨城县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=Residence|1=Dwelling}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の露天風呂|zh|神奈川县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Gifu|ja|岐阜県の露天風呂|zh|岐阜县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Shizuoka|ja|静岡県の露天風呂|zh|静冈县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県の露天風呂|zh|兵库县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|de|Freiluftbad in Hokkaido|ja|北海道の露天風呂|zh|北海道的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Method of Operation === Various types of cameras were employed for the secret filming. In some cases, filming was carried out in the changing room using a {{ill|Subminiature photography|en|Subminiature photography|WD=Q3277847|lt=Small Camera|1=Subminiature photography}}; in others, filming was conducted from over 100 meters away in the mountains with a {{ill|Telephoto lens|en|Telephoto lens|WD=Q516461|lt=Telephoto Camera|1=Telephoto lens}}. There were also instances of "commissioned voyeurism," in which a male customer would bring a desired female to the hot spring and have the waiting Man A record the encounter. In some cases, women were drugged with a {{ill|Date rape drug|en|Date rape drug|WD=Q899702|lt=Date-Rape Drug|1=Date rape drug}} to induce sleep before sexual assault, earning the moniker “Sleeping Princess”. === Characteristics and Countermeasures === The Shizuoka Prefectural Police identified five characteristics commonly found in facilities targeted by the secret filming: # Facilities such as {{ill|Day-Use Bath Facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|zh|日帰浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bath Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} that are frequently visited by young women # An alternation between men’s and women’s baths # An unobstructed line of sight # Locations in the surrounding mountains that allow for filming # The absence of a {{ill|Closed-circuit television camera|en|Closed-circuit television camera|WD=Q5135310|lt=Security Camera|1=Closed-circuit television camera}} at the mountain entrances In response to these vulnerabilities, countermeasures were proposed including the installation of surveillance cameras, modifications to render facilities less visible from the outside, and increased vigilance toward suspicious persons or vehicles. In addition, some inns have installed {{ill|Light-emitting diode|en|Light-emitting diode|WD=Q25504|lt=LED|1=Light-emitting diode}} lighting on the exterior walls of the open-air baths so that the mountain side is illuminated, producing a backlighting effect when viewed from the mountain. == Arrests == The initial arrest of three individuals—including the ringleader Man A—was triggered by a police questioning in October 2021 in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. During the stop-and-question, possession of a saw was discovered; as a result, Man A and his accomplices were arrested for violating the {{ill|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|en|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|WD=Q11650171|lt=Gun and Sword Law|1=Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law}}. Their subsequent explanation—that trees obstructing their filming were cut down—raised suspicions of a nationwide organized secret filming operation. Thereafter, the existence of the voyeurism enthusiast group was confirmed, leading to the successive arrest of its members. Charges include violations of the {{ill|Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防骚扰条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639203}}, as well as offenses under the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} and {{ill|Home invasion|en|Home invasion|WD=Q3560381|lt=Building Trespass|1=Home invasion}}, among other charges. === Ringleader Man A === Man A conspired with other men he met on an adult {{ill|Bulletin board system|en|Bulletin board system|WD=Q210499|lt=Bulletin Board|1=Bulletin board system}} dedicated to sharing voyeurism images, filming female customers at various open-air baths across multiple prefectures. On the internet he came to be known as the '''Voyeurism Icon'''. According to another member of the voyeurism group, Man A was a renowned figure in the realm of open-air bath voyeurism, with many enthusiasts having likely viewed his videos at least once. His secret filming career spanned roughly 30 years; excluding {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, he claimed to have filmed over 10,000 instances across 46 prefectures. It is reported that he sold these videos on voyeurism websites, amassing a considerable sum of money. In 2013, he was arrested by the Ibaraki Prefectural Police for violating the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} after having sold a video featuring a minor. Although Man A often acted alone, during holiday periods—when young women were in greater numbers and the potential for high-quality footage increased—he would form teams for filming excursions. When traveling, he took meticulous precautions such as avoiding highways to obscure travel records, clearing away vegetation the night before filming to prevent obstructions, and even sleeping in his car so as not to leave a hotel record. === List of Arrested Individuals === {| class="wikitable" ! Arrested Person !! Occupation !! Residence !! Arrest Date !! Prefecture}}、{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}、{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}、{{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} |Latitude (degrees)= |Latitude (minutes)= |Latitude (seconds)= |Longitude (degrees)= |Longitude (minutes)= |Longitude (seconds)= |Target= |Date= 2019 – 2021 |Time= |Start time= |End time= |Time zone= |Summary= Incident in which a voyeurism enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers without their consent at open-air baths nationwide. |Reward= |Cause= |Method= |Number of attackers= |Murder weapon= |Weapon= |Armaments= |Deaths= |Injuries= |Missing persons= |Victims= |Damage= |Offender= |Charges= |Motive= |Involvement= |Defense= |Response= |Apology= |Compensation= |Damages= |Criminal proceedings= |Juvenile trial= |Maritime trial= |Civil proceedings= |Impact= |Survivors' association= |Victims' group= |Jurisdiction= }} '''Organized Voyeurism Incident at Open-Air Baths''' (露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件) refers to the case in which, from 2019 to 2021, a {{ill|Secret photography|en|Secret photography|WD=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}} enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers at {{ill|Open-Air Bath|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856214}} facilities nationwide. The secretly filmed videos were shared among the group’s members, and screening events were even held for viewership.[https://jref.com/threads/onsen-voyeurism-17-arrested-over-decades-of-hot-spring-peeping.509953/ Crime - Onsen voyeurism: 17 arrested over decades of hot spring peeping | Japan Reference] Starting with the arrest of three men—including the ringleader referred to as Man A—in December 2021, a total of 16 men have been arrested by the {{ill|Shizuoka Prefectural Police|de|Polizei von Shizuoka|ja|静岡県警察|zh|静冈县警察|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefectural Police|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663257}} as of February 2023 (see {{ill|Arrest|en|Arrest|WD=Q1403016|lt=Later|1=Arrest}}). Although the full scope of the operation remains unclear, over 80 facilities have been affected by the secret filming, more than 1,200 items have been confiscated, and the group is believed to number over 100 members{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/sbs/306002 |title=「沖縄のぞく46都道府県で1万人以上撮った」“盗撮のカリスマ”が供述　マニアグループは総勢100人以上に…SNSやオフ会などで増えたか |accessdate=2023-02-01 |date=2023-02-01 |website=静岡新聞SBS}}. == Summary == === Affected Locations === * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Ibaraki|ja|茨城県の露天風呂|zh|茨城县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=Residence|1=Dwelling}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の露天風呂|zh|神奈川县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Gifu|ja|岐阜県の露天風呂|zh|岐阜县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Shizuoka|ja|静岡県の露天風呂|zh|静冈县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県の露天風呂|zh|兵库县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|de|Freiluftbad in Hokkaido|ja|北海道の露天風呂|zh|北海道的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Method of Operation === Various types of cameras were employed for the secret filming. In some cases, filming was carried out in the changing room using a {{ill|Subminiature photography|en|Subminiature photography|WD=Q3277847|lt=Small Camera|1=Subminiature photography}}; in others, filming was conducted from over 100 meters away in the mountains with a {{ill|Telephoto lens|en|Telephoto lens|WD=Q516461|lt=Telephoto Camera|1=Telephoto lens}}. There were also instances of "commissioned voyeurism," in which a male customer would bring a desired female to the hot spring and have the waiting Man A record the encounter. In some cases, women were drugged with a {{ill|Date rape drug|en|Date rape drug|WD=Q899702|lt=Date-Rape Drug|1=Date rape drug}} to induce sleep before sexual assault, earning the moniker “Sleeping Princess”. === Characteristics and Countermeasures === The Shizuoka Prefectural Police identified five characteristics commonly found in facilities targeted by the secret filming: # Facilities such as {{ill|Day-Use Bath Facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|zh|日帰浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bath Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} that are frequently visited by young women # An alternation between men’s and women’s baths # An unobstructed line of sight # Locations in the surrounding mountains that allow for filming # The absence of a {{ill|Closed-circuit television camera|en|Closed-circuit television camera|WD=Q5135310|lt=Security Camera|1=Closed-circuit television camera}} at the mountain entrances In response to these vulnerabilities, countermeasures were proposed including the installation of surveillance cameras, modifications to render facilities less visible from the outside, and increased vigilance toward suspicious persons or vehicles. In addition, some inns have installed {{ill|Light-emitting diode|en|Light-emitting diode|WD=Q25504|lt=LED|1=Light-emitting diode}} lighting on the exterior walls of the open-air baths so that the mountain side is illuminated, producing a backlighting effect when viewed from the mountain. == Arrests == The initial arrest of three individuals—including the ringleader Man A—was triggered by a police questioning in October 2021 in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. During the stop-and-question, possession of a saw was discovered; as a result, Man A and his accomplices were arrested for violating the {{ill|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|en|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|WD=Q11650171|lt=Gun and Sword Law|1=Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law}}. Their subsequent explanation—that trees obstructing their filming were cut down—raised suspicions of a nationwide organized secret filming operation. Thereafter, the existence of the voyeurism enthusiast group was confirmed, leading to the successive arrest of its members. Charges include violations of the {{ill|Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防骚扰条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639203}}, as well as offenses under the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} and {{ill|Home invasion|en|Home invasion|WD=Q3560381|lt=Building Trespass|1=Home invasion}}, among other charges. === Ringleader Man A === Man A conspired with other men he met on an adult {{ill|Bulletin board system|en|Bulletin board system|WD=Q210499|lt=Bulletin Board|1=Bulletin board system}} dedicated to sharing voyeurism images, filming female customers at various open-air baths across multiple prefectures. On the internet he came to be known as the '''Voyeurism Icon'''. According to another member of the voyeurism group, Man A was a renowned figure in the realm of open-air bath voyeurism, with many enthusiasts having likely viewed his videos at least once. His secret filming career spanned roughly 30 years; excluding {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, he claimed to have filmed over 10,000 instances across 46 prefectures. It is reported that he sold these videos on voyeurism websites, amassing a considerable sum of money. In 2013, he was arrested by the Ibaraki Prefectural Police for violating the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} after having sold a video featuring a minor. Although Man A often acted alone, during holiday periods—when young women were in greater numbers and the potential for high-quality footage increased—he would form teams for filming excursions. When traveling, he took meticulous precautions such as avoiding highways to obscure travel records, clearing away vegetation the night before filming to prevent obstructions, and even sleeping in his car so as not to leave a hotel record. === List of Arrested Individuals === {| class="wikitable" ! Arrested Person !! Occupation !! Residence !! Arrest Date !! Notes/Reference |- | Man A, 49 years old | Unemployed | {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}、{{ill|Namegata|en|Namegata|WD=Q877184|lt=Namegata City|1=Namegata}} | December 2021 | Ringleader{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20211203-OYT1T50070/ |title=「盗撮のカリスマ」ら3人、望遠レンズで露天風呂のぞく…100m超離れて撮影・動画を売買 |accessdate=2022-10-21 |date=2021-12-04 |website=読売新聞オンライン}} |- | Man, 32 years old | Unemployed | {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}、{{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City|1=Okayama}} | December 2021 | 3 years imprisonment, 4 years suspended sentence{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/-/194015 |title=「あなたの感覚は歪んでいる」と裁判官が“説諭”　露天風呂を盗撮　マニアグループの男に有罪判決ー静岡地裁 |accessdate=2022-11-02 |date=2022-11-02 |website=TBS NEWS DIG}} |- | Man, 40 years old | Local government employee

[[ja:露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件]] [[ja:露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件]] [[ja:露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件]] {{Infobox event |Name= Organized Voyeurism Incident at Open-Air Baths |Official name= |image= Shizuoka Prefectural Government Office 20090401.jpg |Caption= Shizuoka Prefectural Police advancing the arrest of the suspect |Location= {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}、{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}、{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}、{{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} |Latitude (degrees)= |Latitude (minutes)= |Latitude (seconds)= |Longitude (degrees)= |Longitude (minutes)= |Longitude (seconds)= |Target= |Date= 2019 – 2021 |Time= |Start time= |End time= |Time zone= |Summary= Incident in which a voyeurism enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers without their consent at open-air baths nationwide. |Reward= |Cause= |Method= |Number of attackers= |Murder weapon= |Weapon= |Armaments= |Deaths= |Injuries= |Missing persons= |Victims= |Damage= |Offender= |Charges= |Motive= |Involvement= |Defense= |Response= |Apology= |Compensation= |Damages= |Criminal proceedings= |Juvenile trial= |Maritime trial= |Civil proceedings= |Impact= |Survivors' association= |Victims' group= |Jurisdiction= }} '''Organized Voyeurism Incident at Open-Air Baths''' (露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件) refers to the case in which, from 2019 to 2021, a {{ill|Secret photography|en|Secret photography|WD=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}} enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers at {{ill|Open-Air Bath|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856214}} facilities nationwide. The secretly filmed videos were shared among the group’s members, and screening events were even held for viewership.[https://jref.com/threads/onsen-voyeurism-17-arrested-over-decades-of-hot-spring-peeping.509953/ Crime - Onsen voyeurism: 17 arrested over decades of hot spring peeping | Japan Reference] Starting with the arrest of three men—including the ringleader referred to as Man A—in December 2021, a total of 16 men have been arrested by the {{ill|Shizuoka Prefectural Police|de|Polizei von Shizuoka|ja|静岡県警察|zh|静冈县警察|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefectural Police|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663257}} as of February 2023 (see {{ill|Arrest|en|Arrest|WD=Q1403016|lt=Later|1=Arrest}}). Although the full scope of the operation remains unclear, over 80 facilities have been affected by the secret filming, more than 1,200 items have been confiscated, and the group is believed to number over 100 members{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/sbs/306002 |title=「沖縄のぞく46都道府県で1万人以上撮った」“盗撮のカリスマ”が供述　マニアグループは総勢100人以上に…SNSやオフ会などで増えたか |accessdate=2023-02-01 |date=2023-02-01 |website=静岡新聞SBS}}. == Summary == === Affected Locations === * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Ibaraki|ja|茨城県の露天風呂|zh|茨城县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=Residence|1=Dwelling}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の露天風呂|zh|神奈川县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Gifu|ja|岐阜県の露天風呂|zh|岐阜县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Shizuoka|ja|静岡県の露天風呂|zh|静冈县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県の露天風呂|zh|兵库县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|de|Freiluftbad in Hokkaido|ja|北海道の露天風呂|zh|北海道的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Method of Operation === Various types of cameras were employed for the secret filming. In some cases, filming was carried out in the changing room using a {{ill|Subminiature photography|en|Subminiature photography|WD=Q3277847|lt=Small Camera|1=Subminiature photography}}; in others, filming was conducted from over 100 meters away in the mountains with a {{ill|Telephoto lens|en|Telephoto lens|WD=Q516461|lt=Telephoto Camera|1=Telephoto lens}}. There were also instances of "commissioned voyeurism," in which a male customer would bring a desired female to the hot spring and have the waiting Man A record the encounter. In some cases, women were drugged with a {{ill|Date rape drug|en|Date rape drug|WD=Q899702|lt=Date-Rape Drug|1=Date rape drug}} to induce sleep before sexual assault, earning the moniker “Sleeping Princess”. === Characteristics and Countermeasures === The Shizuoka Prefectural Police identified five characteristics commonly found in facilities targeted by the secret filming: # Facilities such as {{ill|Day-Use Bath Facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|zh|日帰浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bath Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} that are frequently visited by young women # An alternation between men’s and women’s baths # An unobstructed line of sight # Locations in the surrounding mountains that allow for filming # The absence of a {{ill|Closed-circuit television camera|en|Closed-circuit television camera|WD=Q5135310|lt=Security Camera|1=Closed-circuit television camera}} at the mountain entrances In response to these vulnerabilities, countermeasures were proposed including the installation of surveillance cameras, modifications to render facilities less visible from the outside, and increased vigilance toward suspicious persons or vehicles. In addition, some inns have installed {{ill|Light-emitting diode|en|Light-emitting diode|WD=Q25504|lt=LED|1=Light-emitting diode}} lighting on the exterior walls of the open-air baths so that the mountain side is illuminated, producing a backlighting effect when viewed from the mountain. == Arrests == The initial arrest of three individuals—including the ringleader Man A—was triggered by a police questioning in October 2021 in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. During the stop-and-question, possession of a saw was discovered; as a result, Man A and his accomplices were arrested for violating the {{ill|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|en|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|WD=Q11650171|lt=Gun and Sword Law|1=Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law}}. Their subsequent explanation—that trees obstructing their filming were cut down—raised suspicions of a nationwide organized secret filming operation. Thereafter, the existence of the voyeurism enthusiast group was confirmed, leading to the successive arrest of its members. Charges include violations of the {{ill|Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防骚扰条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639203}}, as well as offenses under the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} and {{ill|Home invasion|en|Home invasion|WD=Q3560381|lt=Building Trespass|1=Home invasion}}, among other charges. === Ringleader Man A === Man A conspired with other men he met on an adult {{ill|Bulletin board system|en|Bulletin board system|WD=Q210499|lt=Bulletin Board|1=Bulletin board system}} dedicated to sharing voyeurism images, filming female customers at various open-air baths across multiple prefectures. On the internet he came to be known as the '''Voyeurism Icon'''. According to another member of the voyeurism group, Man A was a renowned figure in the realm of open-air bath voyeurism, with many enthusiasts having likely viewed his videos at least once. His secret filming career spanned roughly 30 years; excluding {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, he claimed to have filmed over 10,000 instances across 46 prefectures. It is reported that he sold these videos on voyeurism websites, amassing a considerable sum of money. In 2013, he was arrested by the Ibaraki Prefectural Police for violating the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} after having sold a video featuring a minor. Although Man A often acted alone, during holiday periods—when young women were in greater numbers and the potential for high-quality footage increased—he would form teams for filming excursions. When traveling, he took meticulous precautions such as avoiding highways to obscure travel records, clearing away vegetation the night before filming to prevent obstructions, and even sleeping in his car so as not to leave a hotel record. === List of Arrested Individuals === {| class="wikitable" ! Arrested Person !! Occupation !! Residence !! Arrest Date !! Notes/Reference |- | Man A, 49 years old | Unemployed | {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}、{{ill|Namegata|en|Namegata|WD=Q877184|lt=Namegata City|1=Namegata}} | December 2021 | Ringleader{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20211203-OYT1T50070/ |title=「盗撮のカリスマ」ら3人、望遠レンズで露天風呂のぞく…100m超離れて撮影・動画を売買 |accessdate=2022-10-21 |date=2021-12-04 |website=読売新聞オンライン}} |- | Man, 32 years old | Unemployed | {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}、{{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama City|1=Okayama}} | December 2021 | 3 years imprisonment, 4 years suspended sentence{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/-/194015 |title=「あなたの感覚は歪んでいる」と裁判官が“説諭”　露天風呂を盗撮　マニアグループの男に有罪判決ー静岡地裁 |accessdate=2022-11-02 |date=2022-11-02 |website=TBS NEWS DIG}} |- | Man, 40 years old | Local government employee

Prefecture}}、{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}、{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}、{{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}、{{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} |Latitude (degrees)= |Latitude (minutes)= |Latitude (seconds)= |Longitude (degrees)= |Longitude (minutes)= consent at open-air baths nationwide. |Reward= |Cause= |Method= |Number of attackers= |Murder weapon= |Weapon= |Armaments= |Deaths= |Injuries= |Missing persons= |Victims= |Damage= |Offender= |Charges= |Motive= |Involvement= |Defense= |Response= |Apology= |Compensation= |Damages= |Criminal proceedings= |Juvenile trial= |Maritime trial= |Civil proceedings= |Impact= |Survivors' association= |Victims' group= |Jurisdiction= }} '''Organized Voyeurism Incident at Open-Air Baths''' (露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件) refers to the case in which, from 2019 to 2021, a {{ill|Secret photography|en|Secret photography|WD=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}} enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers at {{ill|Open-Air Bath|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856214}} facilities nationwide. The secretly filmed videos were shared among the group’s members, and screening events were even held for viewership.[https://jref.com/threads/onsen-voyeurism-17-arrested-over-decades-of-hot-spring-peeping.509953/ Crime - Onsen voyeurism: 17 arrested over decades of hot spring peeping | Japan Reference] Starting with the arrest of three men—including the ringleader referred to as Man A—in December 2021, a total of 16 men have been arrested by the {{ill|Shizuoka Prefectural Police|de|Polizei von Shizuoka|ja|静岡県警察|zh|静冈县警察|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefectural Police|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663257}} as of February 2023 (see {{ill|Arrest|en|Arrest|WD=Q1403016|lt=Later|1=Arrest}}). Although the full scope of the operation remains unclear, over 80 facilities have been affected by the secret filming, more than 1,200 items have been confiscated, and the group is believed to number over 100 members{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/sbs/306002 |title=「沖縄のぞく46都道府県で1万人以上撮った」“盗撮のカリスマ”が供述　マニアグループは総勢100人以上に…SNSやオフ会などで増えたか |accessdate=2023-02-01 |date=2023-02-01 |website=静岡新聞SBS}}. == Summary == === Affected Locations === * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Ibaraki|ja|茨城県の露天風呂|zh|茨城县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=Residence|1=Dwelling}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の露天風呂|zh|神奈川县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Gifu|ja|岐阜県の露天風呂|zh|岐阜县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Shizuoka|ja|静岡県の露天風呂|zh|静冈县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県の露天風呂|zh|兵库县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|de|Freiluftbad in Hokkaido|ja|北海道の露天風呂|zh|北海道的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Method of Operation === Various types of cameras were employed for the secret filming. In some cases, filming was carried out in the changing room using a {{ill|Subminiature photography|en|Subminiature photography|WD=Q3277847|lt=Small Camera|1=Subminiature photography}}; in others, filming was conducted from over 100 meters away in the mountains with a {{ill|Telephoto lens|en|Telephoto lens|WD=Q516461|lt=Telephoto Camera|1=Telephoto lens}}. There were also instances of "commissioned voyeurism," in which a male customer would bring a desired female to the hot spring and have the waiting Man A record the encounter. In some cases, women were drugged with a {{ill|Date rape drug|en|Date rape drug|WD=Q899702|lt=Date-Rape Drug|1=Date rape drug}} to induce sleep before sexual assault, earning the moniker “Sleeping Princess”. === Characteristics and Countermeasures === The Shizuoka Prefectural Police identified five characteristics commonly found in facilities targeted by the secret filming: # Facilities such as {{ill|Day-Use Bath Facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|zh|日帰浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bath Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} that are frequently visited by young women # An alternation between men’s and women’s baths # An unobstructed line of sight # Locations in the surrounding mountains that allow for filming # The absence of a {{ill|Closed-circuit television camera|en|Closed-circuit television camera|WD=Q5135310|lt=Security Camera|1=Closed-circuit television camera}} at the mountain entrances In response to these vulnerabilities, countermeasures were proposed including the installation of surveillance cameras, modifications to render facilities less visible from the outside, and increased vigilance toward suspicious persons or vehicles. In addition, some inns have installed {{ill|Light-emitting diode|en|Light-emitting diode|WD=Q25504|lt=LED|1=Light-emitting diode}} lighting on the exterior walls of the open-air baths so that the mountain side is illuminated, producing a backlighting effect when viewed from the mountain. == Arrests == The initial arrest of three individuals—including the ringleader Man A—was triggered by a police questioning in October 2021 in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. During the stop-and-question, possession of a saw was discovered; as a result, Man A and his accomplices were arrested for violating the {{ill|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|en|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|WD=Q11650171|lt=Gun and Sword Law|1=Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law}}. Their subsequent explanation—that trees obstructing their filming were cut down—raised suspicions of a nationwide organized secret filming operation. Thereafter, the existence of the voyeurism enthusiast group was confirmed, leading to the successive arrest of its members. Charges include violations of the {{ill|Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防骚扰条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639203}}, as well as offenses under the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} and {{ill|Home invasion|en|Home invasion|WD=Q3560381|lt=Building Trespass|1=Home invasion}}, among other charges. === Ringleader Man A === Man A conspired with other men he met on an adult {{ill|Bulletin board system|en|Bulletin board system|WD=Q210499|lt=Bulletin Board|1=Bulletin board system}} dedicated to sharing voyeurism images, filming female customers at various open-air baths across multiple prefectures. On the internet he came to be known as the '''Voyeurism Icon'''. According to another member of the voyeurism group, Man A was a renowned figure in the realm of open-air bath voyeurism, with many enthusiasts having likely viewed his videos at least once. His secret filming career spanned roughly 30 years; excluding {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, he claimed to have filmed over 10,000 instances across 46 prefectures. It is reported that he sold these videos on voyeurism websites, amassing a considerable sum of money. In 2013, he was arrested by the Ibaraki Prefectural Police for violating the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} after having sold a video featuring a minor. Although Man A often acted alone, during holiday periods—when young women were in greater numbers and the potential for high-quality footage increased—he would form teams for filming excursions. When traveling, he took meticulous precautions such as avoiding highways to obscure travel records, clearing away vegetation the night before filming to prevent obstructions, and even sleeping in his car so as not to leave a hotel record. === List of Arrested Individuals === {| class="wikitable" ! Arrested Person !! Occupation !! Residence !! Arrest Date !! |Jurisdiction= }} '''Organized Voyeurism Incident at Open-Air Baths''' (露天風呂での組織的盗撮事件) refers to the case in which, from 2019 to 2021, a {{ill|Secret photography|en|Secret photography|WD=Q840241|lt=Voyeurism|1=Secret photography}} enthusiast group repeatedly filmed female customers at {{ill|Open-Air Bath|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotenburo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856214}} facilities nationwide. The secretly filmed videos were shared among the group’s members, and screening events were even held for viewership.[https://jref.com/threads/onsen-voyeurism-17-arrested-over-decades-of-hot-spring-peeping.509953/ Crime - Onsen voyeurism: 17 arrested over decades of hot spring peeping | Japan Reference] Starting with the arrest of three men—including the ringleader referred to as Man A—in December 2021, a total of 16 men have been arrested by the {{ill|Shizuoka Prefectural Police|de|Polizei von Shizuoka|ja|静岡県警察|zh|静冈县警察|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefectural Police|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663257}} as of February 2023 (see {{ill|Arrest|en|Arrest|WD=Q1403016|lt=Later|1=Arrest}}). Although the full scope of the operation remains unclear, over 80 facilities have been affected by the secret filming, more than 1,200 items have been confiscated, and the group is believed to number over 100 members{{Cite web|和書|url=https://newsdig.tbs.co.jp/articles/sbs/306002 |title=「沖縄のぞく46都道府県で1万人以上撮った」“盗撮のカリスマ”が供述　マニアグループは総勢100人以上に…SNSやオフ会などで増えたか |accessdate=2023-02-01 |date=2023-02-01 |website=静岡新聞SBS}}. == Summary == === Affected Locations === * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Ibaraki|ja|茨城県の露天風呂|zh|茨城县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Ibaraki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=Residence|1=Dwelling}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の露天風呂|zh|神奈川县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Gifu|ja|岐阜県の露天風呂|zh|岐阜县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Gifu Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Shizuoka|ja|静岡県の露天風呂|zh|静冈县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Shizuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Freiluftbad in Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県の露天風呂|zh|兵库县的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hyōgo Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|de|Freiluftbad in Hokkaido|ja|北海道の露天風呂|zh|北海道的露天浴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-Air Bath in Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} === Method of Operation === Various types of cameras were employed for the secret filming. In some cases, filming was carried out in the changing room using a {{ill|Subminiature photography|en|Subminiature photography|WD=Q3277847|lt=Small Camera|1=Subminiature photography}}; in others, filming was conducted from over 100 meters away in the mountains with a {{ill|Telephoto lens|en|Telephoto lens|WD=Q516461|lt=Telephoto Camera|1=Telephoto lens}}. There were also instances of "commissioned voyeurism," in which a male customer would bring a desired female to the hot spring and have the waiting Man A record the encounter. In some cases, women were drugged with a {{ill|Date rape drug|en|Date rape drug|WD=Q899702|lt=Date-Rape Drug|1=Date rape drug}} to induce sleep before sexual assault, earning the moniker “Sleeping Princess”. === Characteristics and Countermeasures === The Shizuoka Prefectural Police identified five characteristics commonly found in facilities targeted by the secret filming: # Facilities such as {{ill|Day-Use Bath Facility|de|Tagesbad|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|zh|日帰浴设施|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bath Facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} that are frequently visited by young women # An alternation between men’s and women’s baths # An unobstructed line of sight # Locations in the surrounding mountains that allow for filming # The absence of a {{ill|Closed-circuit television camera|en|Closed-circuit television camera|WD=Q5135310|lt=Security Camera|1=Closed-circuit television camera}} at the mountain entrances In response to these vulnerabilities, countermeasures were proposed including the installation of surveillance cameras, modifications to render facilities less visible from the outside, and increased vigilance toward suspicious persons or vehicles. In addition, some inns have installed {{ill|Light-emitting diode|en|Light-emitting diode|WD=Q25504|lt=LED|1=Light-emitting diode}} lighting on the exterior walls of the open-air baths so that the mountain side is illuminated, producing a backlighting effect when viewed from the mountain. == Arrests == The initial arrest of three individuals—including the ringleader Man A—was triggered by a police questioning in October 2021 in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. During the stop-and-question, possession of a saw was discovered; as a result, Man A and his accomplices were arrested for violating the {{ill|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|en|Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law|WD=Q11650171|lt=Gun and Sword Law|1=Firearm and Sword Possession Control Law}}. Their subsequent explanation—that trees obstructing their filming were cut down—raised suspicions of a nationwide organized secret filming operation. Thereafter, the existence of the voyeurism enthusiast group was confirmed, leading to the successive arrest of its members. Charges include violations of the {{ill|Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|de|Belästigungsverordnung|ja|迷惑防止条例|zh|防骚扰条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nuisance Prevention Ordinance|1=Anti-Nuisance Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639203}}, as well as offenses under the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} and {{ill|Home invasion|en|Home invasion|WD=Q3560381|lt=Building Trespass|1=Home invasion}}, among other charges. === Ringleader Man A === Man A conspired with other men he met on an adult {{ill|Bulletin board system|en|Bulletin board system|WD=Q210499|lt=Bulletin Board|1=Bulletin board system}} dedicated to sharing voyeurism images, filming female customers at various open-air baths across multiple prefectures. On the internet he came to be known as the '''Voyeurism Icon'''. According to another member of the voyeurism group, Man A was a renowned figure in the realm of open-air bath voyeurism, with many enthusiasts having likely viewed his videos at least once. His secret filming career spanned roughly 30 years; excluding {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, he claimed to have filmed over 10,000 instances across 46 prefectures. It is reported that he sold these videos on voyeurism websites, amassing a considerable sum of money. In 2013, he was arrested by the Ibaraki Prefectural Police for violating the {{ill|Child pornography laws in Japan|en|Child pornography laws in Japan|WD=Q4251291|lt=Child Pornography Law|1=Child pornography laws in Japan}} after having sold a video featuring a minor. Although Man A often acted alone, during holiday periods—when young women were in greater numbers and the potential for high-quality footage increased—he would form teams for filming excursions. When traveling, he took meticulous precautions such as avoiding highways to obscure travel records, clearing away vegetation the night before filming to prevent obstructions, and even sleeping in his car so as not to leave a hotel record. === List of Arrested Individuals === {| class="wikitable" !
